name: Dominick Profico
summary: Across internships, research, and two years of full-time work, I've built production systems in environments where reliability actually matters. I gravitate toward problems that require understanding something deeply before solving it, and I'm currently looking for a role where that approach is an asset rather than a slow-down.

bullets: Led the migration of 400+ machines from Slackware to Debian, building Ansible and Bash automation that reduced per-machine configuration time from a full day to 30 minutes.
Implemented CIS security compliance across the fleet by developing automated Debian build images and deployment scripts.
Maintained live radar data collection systems across 15 FAA sites, keeping high-availability infrastructure stable on ageing hardware.
Built Python tooling using OpenCV and proprietary image processing logic to automate product defect identification.
Optimised core computational algorithms for mass spectrometry data analysis, achieving a 90% reduction in execution time.
Implemented production data ingestion pipelines in Python, taking research groundwork through to a deployed system.
Researched a knowledge-graph text summarisation algorithm; presented findings at an undergraduate research conference.
Mentored four students through a Python codebase modernisation that improved maintainability across the project.
Built Python and VBA (for Excel) automation tooling to collect test results, generate structured reports, and organise historical data for mission-critical air traffic control systems.
Supported regression testing for ATC systems, including developing new test procedures to recreate and diagnose field issues.

projects.title: Job Hunt Agents
projects.subtitle: Personal project
bullets: Orchestrated Lambda functions through a single SQS consumer with bounded concurrency, pacing LLM calls to stay within provider rate limits and prevent cascading failures.
Built a composable pipeline of single-purpose prompts with structured input/output, chaining summarisation, company research, CV matching, and content generation through Claude Sonnet.
Full-stack on AWS (Lambda · SQS · DynamoDB · API Gateway · Amplify) with a React dashboard for browsing results and triggering on-demand resume tailoring and cover letter generation.

items.name: Python
items.description: My primary language and the one I reach for first. Comfortable across a wide range of applications: automation, data pipelines, computer vision, backend services, and AI integrations. The library list reflects real use, not just familiarity.

items.name: Bash
items.description: My go-to for automation, system configuration, and anything that needs to work reliably at the command line. Comfortable writing scripts that do real work across large numbers of machines.

items.name: C/C++
items.description: Solid academic foundation from my CS degree with project experience outside of coursework. Not my day-to-day language but comfortable reading, reasoning about, and writing it.

items.name: SQL
items.description: Used across multiple roles for querying and data work. Comfortable with the fundamentals, not a specialism.

items.name: Serverless Architecture
items.description: Designing and building event-driven systems on AWS. Comfortable with the full pattern: functions, queues, streams, triggers, and how to make them work together reliably in production.

items.name: Cloud Infrastructure
items.description: Comfortable across the AWS ecosystem beyond just compute. API Gateway, CloudFront, S3, Amplify, SQS, EventBridge. I understand how these services fit together as a system rather than as a list of independent tools.

items.name: Container-based Workflows
items.description: Docker image builds, registry management, and container-based deployments. Comfortable with the full build-push-deploy cycle including the production-specific quirks that don't show up in tutorials.

items.name: Linux/Unix System Administration
items.description: Deep comfort with Linux at the system level. Administration, configuration management, fleet deployment, and keeping production systems stable. Debian and Ubuntu are home, Slackware is in the past.

items.name: Ansible
items.description: Playbook development and fleet-scale configuration management. Comfortable designing automation that works across hundreds of endpoints consistently.

items.name: Anthropic Claude API
items.description: Comfortable with the full API surface: messages, tool use, system prompts, and structured output. Understanding how to get reliable behaviour out of the API rather than just making calls and hoping for the best.

items.name: Prompt Engineering
items.description: Designing prompts that produce consistent, structured output from LLMs. Particularly interested in how prompts behave when agents are doing real work rather than answering one-off questions.

items.name: Git, GitHub, GitLab
items.description: Version control is just part of how I work. Comfortable across all three platforms, branching strategies, and collaborative workflows.

items.name: GitHub Actions
items.description: Building and maintaining CI/CD workflows from scratch. Comfortable with the full pipeline: triggers, jobs, secrets management, and deployment steps.
